common_patterns.overview: Reusable patterns for synthetic-human-tester workflows. These patterns are referenced by the main workflow and provide concrete implementation guidance.

pattern.name: capability_detection
pattern.description: How to detect available MCP servers for each environment type.
pattern.approach: Scan the available MCP tools for name prefixes. Do not hardcode specific server names — any MCP server that provides tools matching the expected patterns can be used.

row.examples: Playwright MCP: browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_snapshot, browser_type, browser_take_screenshot
row.matches: Any MCP with browser_navigate or browser_snapshot

row.examples: Terminal MCP: terminal_spawn, terminal_send, terminal_read. Command group: always available.
row.matches: Prefer Terminal MCP if terminal_spawn exists; fall back to command group

row.examples: Appium MCP: appium_find_element, appium_click, appium_type, appium_get_page_source
row.matches: Any MCP with appium_find_element or appium_get_page_source

row.pattern: exact tool names: Snapshot, Click, Type, Screenshot
row.examples: Windows-MCP: Snapshot, Click, Type, Screenshot, Scroll, Move, Shortcut, Wait, WaitFor, Launch
row.matches: Any MCP with Snapshot AND Click (exact names, no prefix). Type is strongly recommended for text input. Screenshot is separately checked for visual capture — both are optional enhancements.

pattern.name: web_test_execution
pattern.description: Complete flow for executing a single web test step.

step.title: Navigate to Starting State
step.action: If the "given" clause implies a specific page: use browser_navigate to go to the URL. If "given" implies authenticated state and we haven't logged in yet: perform login flow first.

step.title: Pre-Action Snapshot
step.action: Call browser_snapshot to get the current accessibility tree. Verify the target element from the "when" clause exists and is actionable.

step.title: Perform Action
step.action: Based on the "when" clause: - Click: browser_click with the element's role + accessible name - Type: browser_type with the target field and text - Navigate: browser_navigate to the specified URL - Select: browser_select_option for dropdown selections

step.title: Wait for Update
step.action: Allow time for navigation, animation, or async data loading. If the page hasn't updated after the timeout, take a snapshot to see what state we're in.

step.title: Post-Action Snapshot
step.action: Call browser_snapshot to get the updated accessibility tree.

step.title: Verify Assertion
step.action: Compare the post-action snapshot against the "then" clause: - "I should see [text]": search for the text in the snapshot - "I should see [element]": search for the element by role + name - "the [element] should be [state]": verify the element's state (enabled, disabled, checked) - "I should be on [page]": verify the current URL or a heading matches

pattern.name: cli_test_execution
pattern.description: Complete flow for executing a single CLI test step.

step.title: Execute Command
step.action: Parse the "when" clause to extract the command. Execute it with appropriate working directory and environment.

step.title: Capture Results
step.action: Record exit code, stdout, and stderr separately.

step.title: Verify Assertion
step.action: Compare results against the "then" clause: - "exit code is [N]": verify exit code matches - "output contains [text]": search stdout+stderr for the text - "output does not contain [text]": verify text is absent - "output matches [pattern]": verify stdout matches the described pattern

interactive_variant.description: For interactive CLI programs (prompts, TUI). Requires Terminal MCP.
interactive_variant.step: terminal_spawn to start the program in a PTY
terminal_send to respond to the prompt

pattern.name: desktop_test_execution
pattern.description: Complete flow for executing a single desktop test step via Windows-MCP or equivalent.

step.title: Launch Application
step.action: Launch the app using the executable_path from environment config. If launch_args are provided, include them. Wait for the window to appear.

step.title: Pre-Action Snapshot
step.action: Call Snapshot to get the current accessibility tree of the desktop window. Verify the target element from the "when" clause exists and is actionable.

step.title: Perform Action
step.action: Based on the "when" clause: - Click: Click on the element by role + accessible name - Type: Type text into the target field - Select: Select an item from a dropdown or list

step.title: Wait for Update
step.action: Allow time for window transitions, dialog appearance, or UI updates. Desktop apps may animate or load content asynchronously.

step.title: Post-Action Snapshot
step.action: Call Snapshot to get the updated accessibility tree.

step.title: Verify Assertion
step.action: Compare the post-action Snapshot against the "then" clause using the same assertion interpretation as web testing (text_presence, element_state, etc.).

pattern.name: visual_snapshot_capture
pattern.description: Best-effort visual baseline capture before functional testing. Supplementary only — never used for pass/fail decisions.

step.title: Check Screenshot Tool Availability
step.action: Web: check if browser_take_screenshot exists in the detected browser_* tools. Desktop: check if Screenshot exists in the detected desktop tools.

step.title: Capture Visual Baseline
step.action: Web (after auth): call browser_take_screenshot. Desktop (after app launch): call Screenshot. Capture the initial state before any test actions.

step.title: Visual Sanity Check
step.action: If your model can process images: inspect the screenshot for OBVIOUS visual issues only — overlapping elements, text cut off at boundaries, content overflowing viewport, blank/unstyled pages, zero-dimension elements. Do NOT assess subjective design quality.

step.title: Classify Visual Quality
step.action: Assign one of: 🟢 no obvious issues, 🟡 minor issues noted (describe), 🔴 obvious breakage (describe specifically).

pattern.name: report_compilation
pattern.description: How to compile the final structured report for the completion summary.
pattern.template:
STATUS: [all_passed|partial_passed|mcp_unavailable|tests_failed]
VISUAL: [🟢 No obvious issues / 🟡 Minor issues: ... / 🔴 Obvious breakage: ...] [Omitted if no screenshot tool was available]
WEB: [N]/[M] passed [✅/❌/⏭️] [If failed: step description, expected, actual, snapshot excerpt] CLI: [N]/[M] passed [✅/❌/⏭️] [If failed: step description, expected, actual, command output excerpt] NATIVE: [N]/[M] passed [✅/❌/⏭️] [If failed: step description, expected, actual, accessibility tree excerpt] DESKTOP: [N]/[M] passed [✅/❌/⏭️] [If failed: step description, expected, actual, Snapshot excerpt]
SUMMARY: [N] passed, [F] failed, [S] skipped

pattern.name: element_matching
pattern.description: Strategy for finding UI elements in accessibility snapshots.

priority_order.level: 2. Case-insensitive accessible name match: button "submit" matches "Submit"
3. Accessible name contains target text: button "Submit Form" contains "Submit"
4. Role match with nearby text: a button near the text "Submit"
5. Role match only (last resort, ambiguous): first enabled button

pattern.disambiguation: If multiple elements match at the same priority level, prefer: 1. The element that is visible and enabled 2. The element that appears first in reading order 3. If still ambiguous: report the ambiguity and list all matches
